title: Diffusion Models as Continuous Neural Processors
description: [HYPOTHESIS] [AGENT-RESEARCH] Within the Software 2.0 paradigm, Diffusion models are a class of generative processor optimized for continuous probability distributions (e.g., images, video, audio). Instead of autoregressive token prediction, their 'instruction set' involves iteratively denoising a state space. They perform a reverse random walk with drift, typically using a U-Net or Transformer backbone, to map Gaussian noise back to structured data.
